name: Metis
shortDescription: Metis was a Titan goddess of wisdom, prudence, and deep thought. She was the first wife of Zeus, and the mother of Athena.
description: Metis, in Greek mythology, was a Titan goddess revered for her profound wisdom, cunning, and prudence. Her name translates to 'wisdom,' 'skill,' or 'craft.' She was the daughter of Oceanus and Tethys, making her one of the Oceanids. Metis was the first great love and wife of Zeus. A prophecy foretold that Metis would bear two children: a daughter of unmatched power and wisdom, and a son who would overthrow Zeus. Fearing this, Zeus tricked Metis into transforming into a fly, then swallowed her whole. Despite this, Metis continued to live within Zeus, crafting Athena's armor and weapons. Eventually, Zeus developed a terrible headache, and Hephaestus, or Prometheus in some accounts, split open his head with an axe, and Athena sprang forth, fully grown and armed. Metis's influence on Athena is evident in Athena's association with wisdom, strategic warfare, and crafts. While Metis herself doesn't feature prominently in many myths beyond the story of Athena's birth, her role is crucial in shaping the power dynamics of the Olympian pantheon, highlighting the importance of wisdom and foresight, even in the face of divine power.
